title: Artifacts as first-class content — planning overview
description: A scannable visual summary of the artifact-component issue: the embed + reserved-route vision, six settled decisions, the four-source skill convergence, and the seven-workstream plan.

artifact.purpose: Give a reviewer or AI agent a one-screen, scannable overview of the artifact-component issue's planning — the vision, the settled decisions, the skill-convergence map, and the subtask plan — without opening the underlying issue docs.

typography.sans: system-ui humanist stack — headings and prose
typography.mono: ui-monospace stack — eyebrows, chips, ids, routes, source names (the structural voice)

sections: Header — issue title, thesis, status/priority/component chips, theme toggle
The vision — embedded content-area render + /artifacts/<path> full-page route, URL-as-primitive
Settled decisions — the six pinned choices with one-line rationales
Four-source convergence — the four captured skills flowing into artifact-authoring
Subtask plan — the seven workstreams 10->70 with status pills and the suggested build order
decisions: The dedicated /artifacts/<path> URL is the primitive; the embed is built on top of it (the iframe's src IS the route).
Embed affordances: open-full-page is primary (a plain <a href>), in-place expand is a secondary optional affordance.
Metadata lives in a same-name .meta.json/.meta.jsonc sidecar, not injected frontmatter, keeping the HTML pristine and AI-legible.
A reserved-base-URL set (artifacts, assets, content-assets, api, editor) is rejected at config load with a hard throw.
Artifacts serve same-origin and run unsandboxed as first-party content; text/html is scoped to the route, not the shared MIME map.
'Chrome stays' resolves to sidebar-only: an artifact has no headings, so the TOC column collapses and hands its width to the artifact.

sources.note: Every value here is drawn from the listed issue planning docs; nothing is fabricated.
